#### pub fn [send\_transaction](#method.send_transaction)
( &self, transaction: &impl [SerializableTransaction](trait.SerializableTransaction.html "trait solana_client::rpc_client::SerializableTransaction")
, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Signature](https://docs.rs/solana-signature/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_signature/struct.Signature.html "struct solana_signature::Signature")
, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Submits a signed transaction to the network.

Before a transaction is processed, the receiving node runs a “preflight check” which verifies signatures, checks that the node is healthy, and simulates the transaction. If the preflight check fails then an error is returned immediately. Preflight checks can be disabled by calling [`send_transaction_with_config`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.send_transaction_with_config "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::send_transaction_with_config")
 and setting the [`skip_preflight`](../rpc_config/struct.RpcSendTransactionConfig.html#structfield.skip_preflight "field solana_client::rpc_config::RpcSendTransactionConfig::skip_preflight")
 field of [`RpcSendTransactionConfig`](../rpc_config/struct.RpcSendTransactionConfig.html "struct solana_client::rpc_config::RpcSendTransactionConfig")
 to `true`.

This method does not wait for the transaction to be processed or confirmed before returning successfully. To wait for the transaction to be processed or confirmed, use the [`send_and_confirm_transaction`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.send_and_confirm_transaction "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::send_and_confirm_transaction")
 method.

##### [§](#errors-2)
Errors

If the transaction is not signed then an error with kind [`RpcError`](../rpc_request/enum.RpcError.html "enum solana_client::rpc_request::RpcError")
 is returned, containing an [`RpcResponseError`](../rpc_request/enum.RpcError.html#variant.RpcResponseError "variant solana_client::rpc_request::RpcError::RpcResponseError")
 with `code` set to [`JSON_RPC_SERVER_ERROR_TRANSACTION_SIGNATURE_VERIFICATION_FAILURE`](../rpc_custom_error/constant.JSON_RPC_SERVER_ERROR_TRANSACTION_SIGNATURE_VERIFICATION_FAILURE.html "constant solana_client::rpc_custom_error::JSON_RPC_SERVER_ERROR_TRANSACTION_SIGNATURE_VERIFICATION_FAILURE")
.

If the preflight transaction simulation fails then an error with kind [`RpcError`](../rpc_request/enum.RpcError.html "enum solana_client::rpc_request::RpcError")
 is returned, containing an [`RpcResponseError`](../rpc_request/enum.RpcError.html#variant.RpcResponseError "variant solana_client::rpc_request::RpcError::RpcResponseError")
 with `code` set to [`JSON_RPC_SERVER_ERROR_SEND_TRANSACTION_PREFLIGHT_FAILURE`](../rpc_custom_error/constant.JSON_RPC_SERVER_ERROR_SEND_TRANSACTION_PREFLIGHT_FAILURE.html "constant solana_client::rpc_custom_error::JSON_RPC_SERVER_ERROR_SEND_TRANSACTION_PREFLIGHT_FAILURE")
.

If the receiving node is unhealthy, e.g. it is not fully synced to the cluster, then an error with kind [`RpcError`](../rpc_request/enum.RpcError.html "enum solana_client::rpc_request::RpcError")
 is returned, containing an [`RpcResponseError`](../rpc_request/enum.RpcError.html#variant.RpcResponseError "variant solana_client::rpc_request::RpcError::RpcResponseError")
 with `code` set to [`JSON_RPC_SERVER_ERROR_NODE_UNHEALTHY`](../rpc_custom_error/constant.JSON_RPC_SERVER_ERROR_NODE_UNHEALTHY.html "constant solana_client::rpc_custom_error::JSON_RPC_SERVER_ERROR_NODE_UNHEALTHY")
.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-1)
RPC Reference

This method is built on the [`sendTransaction`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/sendtransaction)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-11)
Examples

    // Transfer lamports from Alice to Bob
    let latest_blockhash = rpc_client.get_latest_blockhash()?;
    let tx = system_transaction::transfer(&alice, &bob.pubkey(), lamports, latest_blockhash);
    let signature = rpc_client.send_transaction(&tx)?;

#### pub fn [send\_transaction\_with\_config](#method.send_transaction_with_config)
( &self, transaction: &impl [SerializableTransaction](trait.SerializableTransaction.html "trait solana_client::rpc_client::SerializableTransaction")
, config: [RpcSendTransactionConfig](../rpc_config/struct.RpcSendTransactionConfig.html "struct solana_client::rpc_config::RpcSendTransactionConfig")
, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Signature](https://docs.rs/solana-signature/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_signature/struct.Signature.html "struct solana_signature::Signature")
, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Submits a signed transaction to the network.

Before a transaction is processed, the receiving node runs a “preflight check” which verifies signatures, checks that the node is healthy, and simulates the transaction. If the preflight check fails then an error is returned immediately. Preflight checks can be disabled by setting the [`skip_preflight`](../rpc_config/struct.RpcSendTransactionConfig.html#structfield.skip_preflight "field solana_client::rpc_config::RpcSendTransactionConfig::skip_preflight")
 field of [`RpcSendTransactionConfig`](../rpc_config/struct.RpcSendTransactionConfig.html "struct solana_client::rpc_config::RpcSendTransactionConfig")
 to `true`.

This method does not wait for the transaction to be processed or confirmed before returning successfully. To wait for the transaction to be processed or confirmed, use the [`send_and_confirm_transaction`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.send_and_confirm_transaction "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::send_and_confirm_transaction")
 method.

##### [§](#errors-3)
Errors

If preflight checks are enabled, if the transaction is not signed then an error with kind [`RpcError`](../rpc_request/enum.RpcError.html "enum solana_client::rpc_request::RpcError")
 is returned, containing an [`RpcResponseError`](../rpc_request/enum.RpcError.html#variant.RpcResponseError "variant solana_client::rpc_request::RpcError::RpcResponseError")
 with `code` set to [`JSON_RPC_SERVER_ERROR_TRANSACTION_SIGNATURE_VERIFICATION_FAILURE`](../rpc_custom_error/constant.JSON_RPC_SERVER_ERROR_TRANSACTION_SIGNATURE_VERIFICATION_FAILURE.html "constant solana_client::rpc_custom_error::JSON_RPC_SERVER_ERROR_TRANSACTION_SIGNATURE_VERIFICATION_FAILURE")
.

If preflight checks are enabled, if the preflight transaction simulation fails then an error with kind [`RpcError`](../rpc_request/enum.RpcError.html "enum solana_client::rpc_request::RpcError")
 is returned, containing an [`RpcResponseError`](../rpc_request/enum.RpcError.html#variant.RpcResponseError "variant solana_client::rpc_request::RpcError::RpcResponseError")
 with `code` set to [`JSON_RPC_SERVER_ERROR_SEND_TRANSACTION_PREFLIGHT_FAILURE`](../rpc_custom_error/constant.JSON_RPC_SERVER_ERROR_SEND_TRANSACTION_PREFLIGHT_FAILURE.html "constant solana_client::rpc_custom_error::JSON_RPC_SERVER_ERROR_SEND_TRANSACTION_PREFLIGHT_FAILURE")
.

If the receiving node is unhealthy, e.g. it is not fully synced to the cluster, then an error with kind [`RpcError`](../rpc_request/enum.RpcError.html "enum solana_client::rpc_request::RpcError")
 is returned, containing an [`RpcResponseError`](../rpc_request/enum.RpcError.html#variant.RpcResponseError "variant solana_client::rpc_request::RpcError::RpcResponseError")
 with `code` set to [`JSON_RPC_SERVER_ERROR_NODE_UNHEALTHY`](../rpc_custom_error/constant.JSON_RPC_SERVER_ERROR_NODE_UNHEALTHY.html "constant solana_client::rpc_custom_error::JSON_RPC_SERVER_ERROR_NODE_UNHEALTHY")
.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-2)
RPC Reference

This method is built on the [`sendTransaction`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/sendtransaction)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-12)
Examples

    // Transfer lamports from Alice to Bob
    let latest_blockhash = rpc_client.get_latest_blockhash()?;
    let tx = system_transaction::transfer(&alice, &bob.pubkey(), lamports, latest_blockhash);
    let config = RpcSendTransactionConfig {
        skip_preflight: true,
        .. RpcSendTransactionConfig::default()
    };
    let signature = rpc_client.send_transaction_with_config(
        &tx,
        config,
    )?;

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#867-869)

#### pub fn [send](#method.send)
<T>(&self, request: [RpcRequest](../rpc_request/enum.RpcRequest.html "enum solana_client::rpc_request::RpcRequest")
, params: [Value](https://docs.rs/serde_json/1.0.138/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/serde_json/value/enum.Value.html "enum serde_json::value::Value")
) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<T, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

where T: [DeserializeOwned](https://docs.rs/serde/1.0.217/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/serde/de/trait.DeserializeOwned.html "trait serde::de::DeserializeOwned")
,

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#924)

#### pub fn [confirm\_transaction](#method.confirm_transaction)
(&self, signature: &[Signature](https://docs.rs/solana-signature/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_signature/struct.Signature.html "struct solana_signature::Signature")
) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Check the confirmation status of a transaction.

Returns `true` if the given transaction succeeded and has been committed with the configured [commitment level](https://solana.com/docs/rpc#configuring-state-commitment)
, which can be retrieved with the [`commitment`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.commitment "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::commitment")
 method.

Note that this method does not wait for a transaction to be confirmed — it only checks whether a transaction has been confirmed. To submit a transaction and wait for it to confirm, use [`send_and_confirm_transaction`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.send_and_confirm_transaction "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::send_and_confirm_transaction")
.

_This method returns `false` if the transaction failed, even if it has been confirmed._

##### [§](#rpc-reference-3)
RPC Reference

This method is built on the [`getSignatureStatuses`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getsignaturestatuses)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-13)
Examples

    // Transfer lamports from Alice to Bob and wait for confirmation
    let latest_blockhash = rpc_client.get_latest_blockhash()?;
    let tx = system_transaction::transfer(&alice, &bob.pubkey(), lamports, latest_blockhash);
    let signature = rpc_client.send_transaction(&tx)?;
    
    loop {
        let confirmed = rpc_client.confirm_transaction(&signature)?;
        if confirmed {
            break;
        }
    }

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#980-984)

#### pub fn [confirm\_transaction\_with\_commitment](#method.confirm_transaction_with_commitment)
( &self, signature: &[Signature](https://docs.rs/solana-signature/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_signature/struct.Signature.html "struct solana_signature::Signature")
, commitment\_config: [CommitmentConfig](https://docs.rs/solana-sdk/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_sdk/commitment_config/struct.CommitmentConfig.html "struct solana_sdk::commitment_config::CommitmentConfig")
, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Response](../rpc_response/struct.Response.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::Response")
<[bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
\>, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Check the confirmation status of a transaction.

Returns an [`RpcResult`](../rpc_response/type.RpcResult.html "type solana_client::rpc_response::RpcResult")
 with value `true` if the given transaction succeeded and has been committed with the given [commitment level](https://solana.com/docs/rpc#configuring-state-commitment)
.

Note that this method does not wait for a transaction to be confirmed — it only checks whether a transaction has been confirmed. To submit a transaction and wait for it to confirm, use [`send_and_confirm_transaction`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.send_and_confirm_transaction "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::send_and_confirm_transaction")
.

_This method returns an [`RpcResult`](../rpc_response/type.RpcResult.html "type solana_client::rpc_response::RpcResult")
 with value `false` if the transaction failed, even if it has been confirmed._

##### [§](#rpc-reference-4)
RPC Reference

This method is built on the [`getSignatureStatuses`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getsignaturestatuses)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-14)
Examples

    // Transfer lamports from Alice to Bob and wait for confirmation
    let latest_blockhash = rpc_client.get_latest_blockhash()?;
    let tx = system_transaction::transfer(&alice, &bob.pubkey(), lamports, latest_blockhash);
    let signature = rpc_client.send_transaction(&tx)?;
    
    loop {
        let commitment_config = CommitmentConfig::processed();
        let confirmed = rpc_client.confirm_transaction_with_commitment(&signature, commitment_config)?;
        if confirmed.value {
            break;
        }
    }

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#992-997)

#### pub fn [confirm\_transaction\_with\_spinner](#method.confirm_transaction_with_spinner)
( &self, signature: &[Signature](https://docs.rs/solana-signature/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_signature/struct.Signature.html "struct solana_signature::Signature")
, recent\_blockhash: &[Hash](https://docs.rs/solana-hash/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_hash/struct.Hash.html "struct solana_hash::Hash")
, commitment\_config: [CommitmentConfig](https://docs.rs/solana-sdk/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_sdk/commitment_config/struct.CommitmentConfig.html "struct solana_sdk::commitment_config::CommitmentConfig")
, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[()](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.unit.html)
, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#1059-1062)

#### pub fn [simulate\_transaction](#method.simulate_transaction)
( &self, transaction: &impl [SerializableTransaction](trait.SerializableTransaction.html "trait solana_client::rpc_client::SerializableTransaction")
, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Response](../rpc_response/struct.Response.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::Response")
<[RpcSimulateTransactionResult](../rpc_response/struct.RpcSimulateTransactionResult.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::RpcSimulateTransactionResult")
\>, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Simulates sending a transaction.

If the transaction fails, then the [`err`](../rpc_response/struct.RpcSimulateTransactionResult.html#structfield.err "field solana_client::rpc_response::RpcSimulateTransactionResult::err")
 field of the returned [`RpcSimulateTransactionResult`](../rpc_response/struct.RpcSimulateTransactionResult.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::RpcSimulateTransactionResult")
 will be `Some`. Any logs emitted from the transaction are returned in the [`logs`](../rpc_response/struct.RpcSimulateTransactionResult.html#structfield.logs "field solana_client::rpc_response::RpcSimulateTransactionResult::logs")
 field.

Simulating a transaction is similar to the [“preflight check”](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/sendtransaction)
 that is run by default when sending a transaction.

By default, signatures are not verified during simulation. To verify signatures, call the [`simulate_transaction_with_config`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.simulate_transaction_with_config "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::simulate_transaction_with_config")
 method, with the [`sig_verify`](../rpc_config/struct.RpcSimulateTransactionConfig.html#structfield.sig_verify "field solana_client::rpc_config::RpcSimulateTransactionConfig::sig_verify")
 field of [`RpcSimulateTransactionConfig`](../rpc_config/struct.RpcSimulateTransactionConfig.html "struct solana_client::rpc_config::RpcSimulateTransactionConfig")
 set to `true`.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-5)
RPC Reference

This method is built on the [`simulateTransaction`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/simulatetransaction)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-15)
Examples

    // Transfer lamports from Alice to Bob
    let latest_blockhash = rpc_client.get_latest_blockhash()?;
    let tx = system_transaction::transfer(&alice, &bob.pubkey(), lamports, latest_blockhash);
    let result = rpc_client.simulate_transaction(&tx)?;
    assert!(result.value.err.is_none());

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#1136-1140)

#### pub fn [simulate\_transaction\_with\_config](#method.simulate_transaction_with_config)
( &self, transaction: &impl [SerializableTransaction](trait.SerializableTransaction.html "trait solana_client::rpc_client::SerializableTransaction")
, config: [RpcSimulateTransactionConfig](../rpc_config/struct.RpcSimulateTransactionConfig.html "struct solana_client::rpc_config::RpcSimulateTransactionConfig")
, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Response](../rpc_response/struct.Response.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::Response")
<[RpcSimulateTransactionResult](../rpc_response/struct.RpcSimulateTransactionResult.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::RpcSimulateTransactionResult")
\>, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Simulates sending a transaction.

If the transaction fails, then the [`err`](../rpc_response/struct.RpcSimulateTransactionResult.html#structfield.err "field solana_client::rpc_response::RpcSimulateTransactionResult::err")
 field of the returned [`RpcSimulateTransactionResult`](../rpc_response/struct.RpcSimulateTransactionResult.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::RpcSimulateTransactionResult")
 will be `Some`. Any logs emitted from the transaction are returned in the [`logs`](../rpc_response/struct.RpcSimulateTransactionResult.html#structfield.logs "field solana_client::rpc_response::RpcSimulateTransactionResult::logs")
 field.

Simulating a transaction is similar to the [“preflight check”](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/sendtransaction)
 that is run by default when sending a transaction.

By default, signatures are not verified during simulation. To verify signatures, call the [`simulate_transaction_with_config`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.simulate_transaction_with_config "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::simulate_transaction_with_config")
 method, with the [`sig_verify`](../rpc_config/struct.RpcSimulateTransactionConfig.html#structfield.sig_verify "field solana_client::rpc_config::RpcSimulateTransactionConfig::sig_verify")
 field of [`RpcSimulateTransactionConfig`](../rpc_config/struct.RpcSimulateTransactionConfig.html "struct solana_client::rpc_config::RpcSimulateTransactionConfig")
 set to `true`.

This method can additionally query information about accounts by including them in the [`accounts`](../rpc_config/struct.RpcSimulateTransactionConfig.html#structfield.accounts "field solana_client::rpc_config::RpcSimulateTransactionConfig::accounts")
 field of the [`RpcSimulateTransactionConfig`](../rpc_config/struct.RpcSimulateTransactionConfig.html "struct solana_client::rpc_config::RpcSimulateTransactionConfig")
 argument, in which case those results are reported in the [`accounts`](../rpc_response/struct.RpcSimulateTransactionResult.html#structfield.accounts "field solana_client::rpc_response::RpcSimulateTransactionResult::accounts")
 field of the returned [`RpcSimulateTransactionResult`](../rpc_response/struct.RpcSimulateTransactionResult.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::RpcSimulateTransactionResult")
.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-6)
RPC Reference

This method is built on the [`simulateTransaction`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/simulatetransaction)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-16)
Examples

    // Transfer lamports from Alice to Bob
    let latest_blockhash = rpc_client.get_latest_blockhash()?;
    let tx = system_transaction::transfer(&alice, &bob.pubkey(), lamports, latest_blockhash);
    let config = RpcSimulateTransactionConfig {
        sig_verify: true,
        .. RpcSimulateTransactionConfig::default()
    };
    let result = rpc_client.simulate_transaction_with_config(
        &tx,
        config,
    )?;
    assert!(result.value.err.is_none());

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#1166)

#### pub fn [get\_highest\_snapshot\_slot](#method.get_highest_snapshot_slot)
(&self) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[RpcSnapshotSlotInfo](../rpc_response/struct.RpcSnapshotSlotInfo.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::RpcSnapshotSlotInfo")
, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Returns the highest slot information that the node has snapshots for.

This will find the highest full snapshot slot, and the highest incremental snapshot slot _based on_ the full snapshot slot, if there is one.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-7)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`getHighestSnapshotSlot`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/gethighestsnapshotslot)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-17)
Examples

    let snapshot_slot_info = rpc_client.get_highest_snapshot_slot()?;

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#1222-1225)

#### pub fn [get\_signature\_status](#method.get_signature_status)
( &self, signature: &[Signature](https://docs.rs/solana-signature/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_signature/struct.Signature.html "struct solana_signature::Signature")
, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<[Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[()](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.unit.html)
, [TransactionError](https://docs.rs/solana-transaction-error/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_transaction_error/enum.TransactionError.html "enum solana_transaction_error::TransactionError")
\>>, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Check if a transaction has been processed with the default [commitment level](https://solana.com/docs/rpc#configuring-state-commitment)
.

If the transaction has been processed with the default commitment level, then this method returns `Ok` of `Some`. If the transaction has not yet been processed with the default commitment level, it returns `Ok` of `None`.

If the transaction has been processed with the default commitment level, and the transaction succeeded, this method returns `Ok(Some(Ok(())))`. If the transaction has been processed with the default commitment level, and the transaction failed, this method returns `Ok(Some(Err(_)))`, where the interior error is type [`TransactionError`](https://docs.rs/solana-transaction-error/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_transaction_error/enum.TransactionError.html "enum solana_transaction_error::TransactionError")
.

This function only searches a node’s recent history, including all recent slots, plus up to [`MAX_RECENT_BLOCKHASHES`](https://docs.rs/solana-clock/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_clock/constant.MAX_RECENT_BLOCKHASHES.html "constant solana_clock::MAX_RECENT_BLOCKHASHES")
 rooted slots. To search the full transaction history use the [`get_signature_status_with_commitment_and_history`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.get_signature_status_with_commitment_and_history "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::get_signature_status_with_commitment_and_history")
 method.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-8)
RPC Reference

This method is built on the [`getSignatureStatuses`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getsignaturestatuses)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-18)
Examples

    let signature = rpc_client.send_transaction(&tx)?;
    let status = rpc_client.get_signature_status(&signature)?;

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#1298-1301)

#### pub fn [get\_signature\_statuses](#method.get_signature_statuses)
( &self, signatures: &\[[Signature](https://docs.rs/solana-signature/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_signature/struct.Signature.html "struct solana_signature::Signature")\
\], ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Response](../rpc_response/struct.Response.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::Response")
<[Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<[Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<[TransactionStatus](https://docs.rs/solana-transaction-status-client-types/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_transaction_status_client_types/struct.TransactionStatus.html "struct solana_transaction_status_client_types::TransactionStatus")
\>>>, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Gets the statuses of a list of transaction signatures.

The returned vector of [`TransactionStatus`](https://docs.rs/solana-transaction-status-client-types/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_transaction_status_client_types/struct.TransactionStatus.html "struct solana_transaction_status_client_types::TransactionStatus")
 has the same length as the input slice.

For any transaction that has not been processed by the network, the value of the corresponding entry in the returned vector is `None`. As a result, a transaction that has recently been submitted will not have a status immediately.

To submit a transaction and wait for it to confirm, use [`send_and_confirm_transaction`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.send_and_confirm_transaction "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::send_and_confirm_transaction")
.

This function ignores the configured confirmation level, and returns the transaction status whatever it is. It does not wait for transactions to be processed.

This function only searches a node’s recent history, including all recent slots, plus up to [`MAX_RECENT_BLOCKHASHES`](https://docs.rs/solana-clock/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_clock/constant.MAX_RECENT_BLOCKHASHES.html "constant solana_clock::MAX_RECENT_BLOCKHASHES")
 rooted slots. To search the full transaction history use the [`get_signature_statuses_with_history`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.get_signature_statuses_with_history "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::get_signature_statuses_with_history")
 method.

##### [§](#errors-4)
Errors

Any individual `TransactionStatus` may have triggered an error during processing, in which case its [`err`](https://docs.rs/solana-transaction-status-client-types/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_transaction_status_client_types/struct.TransactionStatus.html#structfield.err "field solana_transaction_status_client_types::TransactionStatus::err")
 field will be `Some`.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-9)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`getSignatureStatuses`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getsignaturestatuses)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-19)
Examples

    // Send lamports from Alice to Bob and wait for the transaction to be processed
    let latest_blockhash = rpc_client.get_latest_blockhash()?;
    let tx = system_transaction::transfer(&alice, &bob.pubkey(), lamports, latest_blockhash);
    let signature = rpc_client.send_transaction(&tx)?;
    
    let status = loop {
       let statuses = rpc_client.get_signature_statuses(&[signature])?.value;
       if let Some(status) = statuses[0].clone() {
           break status;
       }
       std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100));
    };
    
    assert!(status.err.is_none());

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#1364-1367)

#### pub fn [get\_signature\_statuses\_with\_history](#method.get_signature_statuses_with_history)
( &self, signatures: &\[[Signature](https://docs.rs/solana-signature/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_signature/struct.Signature.html "struct solana_signature::Signature")\
\], ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Response](../rpc_response/struct.Response.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::Response")
<[Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<[Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<[TransactionStatus](https://docs.rs/solana-transaction-status-client-types/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_transaction_status_client_types/struct.TransactionStatus.html "struct solana_transaction_status_client_types::TransactionStatus")
\>>>, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Gets the statuses of a list of transaction signatures.

The returned vector of [`TransactionStatus`](https://docs.rs/solana-transaction-status-client-types/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_transaction_status_client_types/struct.TransactionStatus.html "struct solana_transaction_status_client_types::TransactionStatus")
 has the same length as the input slice.

For any transaction that has not been processed by the network, the value of the corresponding entry in the returned vector is `None`. As a result, a transaction that has recently been submitted will not have a status immediately.

To submit a transaction and wait for it to confirm, use [`send_and_confirm_transaction`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.send_and_confirm_transaction "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::send_and_confirm_transaction")
.

This function ignores the configured confirmation level, and returns the transaction status whatever it is. It does not wait for transactions to be processed.

This function searches a node’s full ledger history and (if implemented) long-term storage. To search for transactions in recent slots only use the [`get_signature_statuses`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.get_signature_statuses "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::get_signature_statuses")
 method.

##### [§](#errors-5)
Errors

Any individual `TransactionStatus` may have triggered an error during processing, in which case its [`err`](https://docs.rs/solana-transaction-status-client-types/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_transaction_status_client_types/struct.TransactionStatus.html#structfield.err "field solana_transaction_status_client_types::TransactionStatus::err")
 field will be `Some`.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-10)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`getSignatureStatuses`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getsignaturestatuses)
 RPC method, with the `searchTransactionHistory` configuration option set to `true`.

##### [§](#examples-20)
Examples

    // Check if an old transaction exists
    let signature = get_old_transaction_signature();
    let latest_blockhash = rpc_client.get_latest_blockhash()?;
    let statuses = rpc_client.get_signature_statuses_with_history(&[signature])?.value;
    if statuses[0].is_none() {
        println!("old transaction does not exist");
    }

\>

Check if a transaction has been processed with the given [commitment level](https://solana.com/docs/rpc#configuring-state-commitment)
.

If the transaction has been processed with the given commitment level, then this method returns `Ok` of `Some`. If the transaction has not yet been processed with the given commitment level, it returns `Ok` of `None`.

If the transaction has been processed with the given commitment level, and the transaction succeeded, this method returns `Ok(Some(Ok(())))`. If the transaction has been processed with the given commitment level, and the transaction failed, this method returns `Ok(Some(Err(_)))`, where the interior error is type [`TransactionError`](https://docs.rs/solana-transaction-error/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_transaction_error/enum.TransactionError.html "enum solana_transaction_error::TransactionError")
.

This function only searches a node’s recent history, including all recent slots, plus up to [`MAX_RECENT_BLOCKHASHES`](https://docs.rs/solana-clock/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_clock/constant.MAX_RECENT_BLOCKHASHES.html "constant solana_clock::MAX_RECENT_BLOCKHASHES")
 rooted slots. To search the full transaction history use the [`get_signature_status_with_commitment_and_history`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.get_signature_status_with_commitment_and_history "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::get_signature_status_with_commitment_and_history")
 method.

.

If the transaction has been processed with the given commitment level, then this method returns `Ok` of `Some`. If the transaction has not yet been processed with the given commitment level, it returns `Ok` of `None`.

If the transaction has been processed with the given commitment level, and the transaction succeeded, this method returns `Ok(Some(Ok(())))`. If the transaction has been processed with the given commitment level, and the transaction failed, this method returns `Ok(Some(Err(_)))`, where the interior error is type [`TransactionError`](https://docs.rs/solana-transaction-error/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_transaction_error/enum.TransactionError.html "enum solana_transaction_error::TransactionError")

#### pub fn [get\_block\_production\_with\_config](#method.get_block_production_with_config)
( &self, config: [RpcBlockProductionConfig](../rpc_config/struct.RpcBlockProductionConfig.html "struct solana_client::rpc_config::RpcBlockProductionConfig")
, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Response](../rpc_response/struct.Response.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::Response")
<[RpcBlockProduction](../rpc_response/struct.RpcBlockProduction.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::RpcBlockProduction")
\>, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Get block production for the current or previous epoch.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-19)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`getBlockProduction`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getblockproduction)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-29)
Examples

    let leader = rpc_client.get_slot_leaders(start_slot, limit)?;
    let leader = leader[0];
    let range = RpcBlockProductionConfigRange {
        first_slot: start_slot,
        last_slot: Some(start_slot + limit),
    };
    let config = RpcBlockProductionConfig {
        identity: Some(leader.to_string()),
        range: Some(range),
        commitment: Some(CommitmentConfig::processed()),
    };
    let production = rpc_client.get_block_production_with_config(
        config
    )?;

        sort_results: None,
    };

        config,

#### pub fn [get\_block](#method.get_block)
(&self, slot: [u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html)
) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[EncodedConfirmedBlock](https://docs.rs/solana-transaction-status-client-types/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_transaction_status_client_types/struct.EncodedConfirmedBlock.html "struct solana_transaction_status_client_types::EncodedConfirmedBlock")
, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Returns identity and transaction information about a confirmed block in the ledger.

The encodings are returned in [`UiTransactionEncoding::Json`](https://docs.rs/solana-transaction-status-client-types/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_transaction_status_client_types/enum.UiTransactionEncoding.html#variant.Json "variant solana_transaction_status_client_types::UiTransactionEncoding::Json")
 format. To return transactions in other encodings, use [`get_block_with_encoding`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.get_block_with_encoding "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::get_block_with_encoding")
.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-27)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`getBlock`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getblock)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-37)
Examples

    let block = rpc_client.get_block(slot)?;

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#1993-1997)

    let config = RpcBlockConfig {

        transaction_details: Some(TransactionDetails::None),
        rewards: Some(true),

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#2084)

#### pub fn [get\_blocks](#method.get_blocks)
( &self, start\_slot: [u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html)
, end\_slot: [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<[u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html)
\>, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<[u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html)
\>, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Returns a list of finalized blocks between two slots.

The range is inclusive, with results including the block for both `start_slot` and `end_slot`.

If `end_slot` is not provided, then the end slot is for the latest finalized block.

This method may not return blocks for the full range of slots if some slots do not have corresponding blocks. To simply get a specific number of sequential blocks, use the [`get_blocks_with_limit`](RpcClient::get_blocks_with_limit.)
 method.

This method uses the [`Finalized`](https://docs.rs/solana-sdk/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_sdk/commitment_config/enum.CommitmentLevel.html#variant.Finalized "variant solana_sdk::commitment_config::CommitmentLevel::Finalized")
 [commitment level](https://solana.com/docs/rpc#configuring-state-commitment)
.

##### [§](#errors-6)
Errors

This method returns an error if the range is greater than 500,000 slots.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-30)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`getBlocks`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getblocks)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-40)
Examples

    // Get up to the first 10 blocks
    let start_slot = 0;
    let end_slot = 9;
    let blocks = rpc_client.get_blocks(start_slot, Some(end_slot))?;

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#2139-2144)

#### pub fn [get\_blocks\_with\_commitment](#method.get_blocks_with_commitment)
( &self, start\_slot: [u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html)
, end\_slot: [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<[u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html)
\>, commitment\_config: [CommitmentConfig](https://docs.rs/solana-sdk/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_sdk/commitment_config/struct.CommitmentConfig.html "struct solana_sdk::commitment_config::CommitmentConfig")
, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<[u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html)
\>, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Returns a list of confirmed blocks between two slots.

The range is inclusive, with results including the block for both `start_slot` and `end_slot`.

If `end_slot` is not provided, then the end slot is for the latest block with the given [commitment level](https://solana.com/docs/rpc#configuring-state-commitment)
.

This method may not return blocks for the full range of slots if some slots do not have corresponding blocks. To simply get a specific number of sequential blocks, use the [`get_blocks_with_limit_and_commitment`](RpcClient::get_blocks_with_limit_and_commitment.)
 method.

##### [§](#errors-7)
Errors

This method returns an error if the range is greater than 500,000 slots.

This method returns an error if the given commitment level is below [`Confirmed`](https://docs.rs/solana-sdk/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_sdk/commitment_config/enum.CommitmentLevel.html#variant.Confirmed "variant solana_sdk::commitment_config::CommitmentLevel::Confirmed")
.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-31)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`getBlocks`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getblocks)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-41)
Examples

    // Get up to the first 10 blocks
    let start_slot = 0;
    let end_slot = 9;
    // Method does not support commitment below `confirmed`
    let commitment_config = CommitmentConfig::confirmed();
    let blocks = rpc_client.get_blocks_with_commitment(
        start_slot,
        Some(end_slot),
        commitment_config,
    )?;

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#2182)

#### pub fn [get\_blocks\_with\_limit](#method.get_blocks_with_limit)
( &self, start\_slot: [u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html)
, limit: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")

<[u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html)
\>, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Returns a list of finalized blocks starting at the given slot.

This method uses the [`Finalized`](CommitmentLevel::Finalized.)
 [commitment level](https://solana.com/docs/rpc#configuring-state-commitment)
.

##### [§](#errors-8)
Errors

This method returns an error if the limit is greater than 500,000 slots.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-32)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`getBlocksWithLimit`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getblockswithlimit)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-42)
Examples

    // Get the first 10 blocks
    let start_slot = 0;
    let limit = 10;
    let blocks = rpc_client.get_blocks_with_limit(start_slot, limit)?;

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#2223-2228)

#### pub fn [get\_blocks\_with\_limit\_and\_commitment](#method.get_blocks_with_limit_and_commitment)
( &self, start\_slot: [u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html)
, limit: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
, commitment\_config: [CommitmentConfig](https://docs.rs/solana-sdk/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_sdk/commitment_config/struct.CommitmentConfig.html "struct solana_sdk::commitment_config::CommitmentConfig")
, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<[u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html)
\>, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Returns a list of confirmed blocks starting at the given slot.

##### [§](#errors-9)
Errors

This method returns an error if the limit is greater than 500,000 slots.

This method returns an error if the given [commitment level](https://solana.com/docs/rpc#configuring-state-commitment)
 is below [`Confirmed`](https://docs.rs/solana-sdk/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_sdk/commitment_config/enum.CommitmentLevel.html#variant.Confirmed "variant solana_sdk::commitment_config::CommitmentLevel::Confirmed")
.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-33)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`getBlocksWithLimit`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getblockswithlimit)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-43)
Examples

    // Get the first 10 blocks
    let start_slot = 0;
    let limit = 10;
    let commitment_config = CommitmentConfig::confirmed();
    let blocks = rpc_client.get_blocks_with_limit_and_commitment(
        start_slot,
        limit,
        commitment_config,
    )?;

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#2271-2274)

#### pub fn [get\_signatures\_for\_address](#method.get_signatures_for_address)
( &self, address: &[Pubkey](https://docs.rs/solana-pubkey/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_pubkey/struct.Pubkey.html "struct solana_pubkey::Pubkey")
, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<[RpcConfirmedTransactionStatusWithSignature](../rpc_response/struct.RpcConfirmedTransactionStatusWithSignature.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::RpcConfirmedTransactionStatusWithSignature")
\>, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Get confirmed signatures for transactions involving an address.

Returns up to 1000 signatures, ordered from newest to oldest.

This method uses the [`Finalized`](CommitmentLevel::Finalized.)
 [commitment level](https://solana.com/docs/rpc#configuring-state-commitment)
.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-34)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`getSignaturesForAddress`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getsignaturesforaddress)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-44)
Examples

    let signatures = rpc_client.get_signatures_for_address(
        &alice.pubkey(),
    )?;

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#2325-2329)

#### pub fn [get\_signatures\_for\_address\_with\_config](#method.get_signatures_for_address_with_config)
( &self, address: &[Pubkey](https://docs.rs/solana-pubkey/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_pubkey/struct.Pubkey.html "struct solana_pubkey::Pubkey")
, config: [GetConfirmedSignaturesForAddress2Config](struct.GetConfirmedSignaturesForAddress2Config.html "struct solana_client::rpc_client::GetConfirmedSignaturesForAddress2Config")
, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<[RpcConfirmedTransactionStatusWithSignature](../rpc_response/struct.RpcConfirmedTransactionStatusWithSignature.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::RpcConfirmedTransactionStatusWithSignature")
\>, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Get confirmed signatures for transactions involving an address.

##### [§](#errors-10)
Errors

This method returns an error if the given [commitment level](https://solana.com/docs/rpc#configuring-state-commitment)
 is below [`Confirmed`](https://docs.rs/solana-sdk/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_sdk/commitment_config/enum.CommitmentLevel.html#variant.Confirmed "variant solana_sdk::commitment_config::CommitmentLevel::Confirmed")
.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-35)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`getSignaturesForAddress`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getsignaturesforaddress)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-45)
Examples

    let config = GetConfirmedSignaturesForAddress2Config {
        before: None,
        until: None,
        limit: Some(3),
        commitment: Some(CommitmentConfig::confirmed()),
    };
    let signatures = rpc_client.get_signatures_for_address_with_config(
        &alice.pubkey(),
        config,
    )?;

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#2373-2377)

#### pub fn [get\_transaction](#method.get_transaction)
( &self, signature: &[Signature](https://docs.rs/solana-signature/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_signature/struct.Signature.html "struct solana_signature::Signature")
, encoding: [UiTransactionEncoding](https://docs.rs/solana-transaction-status-client-types/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_transaction_status_client_types/enum.UiTransactionEncoding.html "enum solana_transaction_status_client_types::UiTransactionEncoding")
, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[EncodedConfirmedTransactionWithStatusMeta](https://docs.rs/solana-transaction-status-client-types/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_transaction_status_client_types/struct.EncodedConfirmedTransactionWithStatusMeta.html "struct solana_transaction_status_client_types::EncodedConfirmedTransactionWithStatusMeta")
, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Returns transaction details for a confirmed transaction.

This method uses the [`Finalized`](https://docs.rs/solana-sdk/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_sdk/commitment_config/enum.CommitmentLevel.html#variant.Finalized "variant solana_sdk::commitment_config::CommitmentLevel::Finalized")
 [commitment level](https://solana.com/docs/rpc#configuring-state-commitment)
.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-36)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`getTransaction`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/gettransaction)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-46)
Examples

    let signature = rpc_client.send_and_confirm_transaction(&tx)?;
    let transaction = rpc_client.get_transaction(
        &signature,
        UiTransactionEncoding::Json,
    )?;

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#2431-2435)

#### pub fn [get\_transaction\_with\_config](#method.get_transaction_with_config)
( &self, signature: &[Signature](https://docs.rs/solana-signature/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_signature/struct.Signature.html "struct solana_signature::Signature")
, config: [RpcTransactionConfig](../rpc_config/struct.RpcTransactionConfig.html "struct solana_client::rpc_config::RpcTransactionConfig")
, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[EncodedConfirmedTransactionWithStatusMeta](https://docs.rs/solana-transaction-status-client-types/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_transaction_status_client_types/struct.EncodedConfirmedTransactionWithStatusMeta.html "struct solana_transaction_status_client_types::EncodedConfirmedTransactionWithStatusMeta")
, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Returns transaction details for a confirmed transaction.

##### [§](#errors-11)
Errors

This method returns an error if the given [commitment level](https://solana.com/docs/rpc#configuring-state-commitment)
 is below [`Confirmed`](https://docs.rs/solana-sdk/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_sdk/commitment_config/enum.CommitmentLevel.html#variant.Confirmed "variant solana_sdk::commitment_config::CommitmentLevel::Confirmed")
.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-37)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`getTransaction`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/gettransaction)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-47)
Examples

    let signature = rpc_client.send_and_confirm_transaction(&tx)?;
    let config = RpcTransactionConfig {
        encoding: Some(UiTransactionEncoding::Json),
        commitment: Some(CommitmentConfig::confirmed()),
        max_supported_transaction_version: Some(0),
    };
    let transaction = rpc_client.get_transaction_with_config(
        &signature,
        config,
    )?;

#### pub fn [get\_recent\_performance\_samples](#method.get_recent_performance_samples)
( &self, limit: [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<[usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
\>, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<[RpcPerfSample](../rpc_response/struct.RpcPerfSample.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::RpcPerfSample")
\>, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Returns a list of recent performance samples, in reverse slot order.

Performance samples are taken every 60 seconds and include the number of transactions and slots that occur in a given time window.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-45)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`getRecentPerformanceSamples`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getrecentperformancesamples)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-55)
Examples

    let limit = 10;
    let performance_samples = rpc_client.get_recent_performance_samples(
        Some(limit),
    )?;

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#2697-2700)

#### pub fn [get\_recent\_prioritization\_fees](#method.get_recent_prioritization_fees)
( &self, addresses: &\[[Pubkey](https://docs.rs/solana-pubkey/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_pubkey/struct.Pubkey.html "struct solana_pubkey::Pubkey")\
\], ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<[RpcPrioritizationFee](../rpc_response/struct.RpcPrioritizationFee.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::RpcPrioritizationFee")
\>, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Returns a list of minimum prioritization fees from recent blocks. Takes an optional vector of addresses; if any addresses are provided, the response will reflect the minimum prioritization fee to land a transaction locking all of the provided accounts as writable.

Currently, a node’s prioritization-fee cache stores data from up to 150 blocks.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-46)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`getRecentPrioritizationFees`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getrecentprioritizationfees)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-56)
Examples

    let addresses = vec![alice.pubkey(), bob.pubkey()];
    let prioritization_fees = rpc_client.get_recent_prioritization_fees(
        &addresses,
    )?;

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#2721)

\>>, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Returns the inflation reward for a list of addresses for an epoch.

This method uses the configured [commitment level](https://solana.com/docs/rpc#configuring-state-commitment)
.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-50)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`getInflationReward`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getinflationreward)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-60)
Examples

    let addresses = vec![alice.pubkey(), bob.pubkey()];
    let inflation_reward = rpc_client.get_inflation_reward(
        &addresses,
        Some(epoch),
    )?;

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#2832)

#### pub fn [get\_version](#method.get_version)
(&self) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[RpcVersionInfo](../rpc_response/struct.RpcVersionInfo.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::RpcVersionInfo")
, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Returns the current solana version running on the node.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-51)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`getVersion`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getversion)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-61)
Examples

    let expected_version = semver::Version::new(1, 7, 0);
    let version = rpc_client.get_version()?;
    let version = semver::Version::parse(&version.solana_core)?;
    assert!(version >= expected_version);

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#2857)

#### pub fn [minimum\_ledger\_slot](#method.minimum_ledger_slot)
(&self) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html)
, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Returns the lowest slot that the node has information about in its ledger.

This value may increase over time if the node is configured to purge older ledger data.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-52)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`minimumLedgerSlot`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/minimumledgerslot)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-62)
Examples

    let slot = rpc_client.minimum_ledger_slot()?;

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#2903)

#### pub fn [get\_account](#method.get_account)
(&self, pubkey: &[Pubkey](https://docs.rs/solana-pubkey/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_pubkey/struct.Pubkey.html "struct solana_pubkey::Pubkey")
) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Account](https://docs.rs/solana-account/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_account/struct.Account.html "struct solana_account::Account")
, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Returns all information associated with the account of the provided pubkey.

This method uses the configured [commitment level](https://solana.com/docs/rpc#configuring-state-commitment)
.

To get multiple accounts at once, use the [`get_multiple_accounts`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.get_multiple_accounts "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::get_multiple_accounts")
 method.

##### [§](#errors-12)
Errors

If the account does not exist, this method returns [`RpcError::ForUser`](../rpc_request/enum.RpcError.html#variant.ForUser "variant solana_client::rpc_request::RpcError::ForUser")
. This is unlike [`get_account_with_commitment`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.get_account_with_commitment "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::get_account_with_commitment")
, which returns `Ok(None)` if the account does not exist.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-53)
RPC Reference

This method is built on the [`getAccountInfo`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getaccountinfo)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-63)
Examples

    let alice_pubkey = Pubkey::from_str("BgvYtJEfmZYdVKiptmMjxGzv8iQoo4MWjsP3QsTkhhxa").unwrap();
    let account = rpc_client.get_account(&alice_pubkey)?;

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#2944-2948)

#### pub fn [get\_account\_with\_commitment](#method.get_account_with_commitment)
( &self, pubkey: &[Pubkey](https://docs.rs/solana-pubkey/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_pubkey/struct.Pubkey.html "struct solana_pubkey::Pubkey")
, commitment\_config: [CommitmentConfig](https://docs.rs/solana-sdk/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_sdk/commitment_config/struct.CommitmentConfig.html "struct solana_sdk::commitment_config::CommitmentConfig")
, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Response](../rpc_response/struct.Response.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::Response")
<[Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<[Account](https://docs.rs/solana-account/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_account/struct.Account.html "struct solana_account::Account")
\>>, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Returns all information associated with the account of the provided pubkey.

If the account does not exist, this method returns `Ok(None)`.

To get multiple accounts at once, use the [`get_multiple_accounts_with_commitment`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.get_multiple_accounts_with_commitment "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::get_multiple_accounts_with_commitment")
 method.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-54)
RPC Reference

This method is built on the [`getAccountInfo`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getaccountinfo)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-64)
Examples

    let alice_pubkey = Pubkey::from_str("BgvYtJEfmZYdVKiptmMjxGzv8iQoo4MWjsP3QsTkhhxa").unwrap();
    let commitment_config = CommitmentConfig::processed();
    let account = rpc_client.get_account_with_commitment(
        &alice_pubkey,
        commitment_config,
    )?;
    assert!(account.value.is_some());

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#3000-3004)

#### pub fn [get\_account\_with\_config](#method.get_account_with_config)
( &self, pubkey: &[Pubkey](https://docs.rs/solana-pubkey/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_pubkey/struct.Pubkey.html "struct solana_pubkey::Pubkey")
, config: [RpcAccountInfoConfig](../rpc_config/struct.RpcAccountInfoConfig.html "struct solana_client::rpc_config::RpcAccountInfoConfig")
, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Response](../rpc_response/struct.Response.html "struct solana_client::rpc_response::Response")
<[Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<[Account](https://docs.rs/solana-account/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_account/struct.Account.html "struct solana_account::Account")
\>>, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Returns all information associated with the account of the provided pubkey.

If the account does not exist, this method returns `Ok(None)`.

To get multiple accounts at once, use the [`get_multiple_accounts_with_config`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.get_multiple_accounts_with_config "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::get_multiple_accounts_with_config")
 method.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-55)
RPC Reference

This method is built on the [`getAccountInfo`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getaccountinfo)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-65)
Examples

    let alice_pubkey = Pubkey::from_str("BgvYtJEfmZYdVKiptmMjxGzv8iQoo4MWjsP3QsTkhhxa").unwrap();
    let commitment_config = CommitmentConfig::processed();
    let config = RpcAccountInfoConfig {
        encoding: Some(UiAccountEncoding::Base64),
        commitment: Some(commitment_config),
        .. RpcAccountInfoConfig::default()
    };
    let account = rpc_client.get_account_with_config(
        &alice_pubkey,
        config,
    )?;
    assert!(account.value.is_some());

#### pub fn [get\_account\_data](#method.get_account_data)
(&self, pubkey: &[Pubkey](https://docs.rs/solana-pubkey/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_pubkey/struct.Pubkey.html "struct solana_pubkey::Pubkey")

<[Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<[u8](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html)
\>, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Gets the raw data associated with an account.

This is equivalent to calling [`get_account`](struct.RpcClient.html#method.get_account "method solana_client::rpc_client::RpcClient::get_account")
 and then accessing the [`data`](https://docs.rs/solana-account/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_account/struct.Account.html#method.data "method solana_account::Account::data")
 field of the returned [`Account`](https://docs.rs/solana-account/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_account/struct.Account.html "struct solana_account::Account")
.

 RPC method.

Examples

    let memcmp = RpcFilterType::Memcmp(Memcmp::new(
        0,                                                    // offset
        MemcmpEncodedBytes::Base64(base64_bytes.to_string()), // encoded bytes
    ));
    let config = RpcProgramAccountsConfig {
        filters: Some(vec![\
            RpcFilterType::DataSize(128),\
            memcmp,\
        ]),
        account_config: RpcAccountInfoConfig {
            encoding: Some(UiAccountEncoding::Base64),
            data_slice: Some(UiDataSliceConfig {
                offset: 0,
                length: 5,
            }),
            commitment: Some(CommitmentConfig::processed()),
            min_context_slot: Some(1234),
        },
        with_context: Some(false),
        sort_results: Some(true),
    };
    let accounts = rpc_client.get_program_accounts_with_config(
        &alice.pubkey(),
        config,
    )?;

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#3405)

#### pub fn [get\_stake\_minimum\_delegation](#method.get_stake_minimum_delegation)
(&self) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html)
, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Returns the stake minimum delegation, in lamports.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-67)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`getStakeMinimumDelegation`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getstakeminimumdelegation)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-77)
Examples

    let stake_minimum_delegation = rpc_client.get_stake_minimum_delegation()?;

[Source](https://docs.rs/solana-rpc-client/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/solana_rpc_client/rpc_client.rs.html#3428-3431)

#### pub fn [get\_stake\_minimum\_delegation\_with\_commitment](#method.get_stake_minimum_delegation_with_commitment)
( &self, commitment\_config: [CommitmentConfig](https://docs.rs/solana-sdk/2.1.13/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/solana_sdk/commitment_config/struct.CommitmentConfig.html "struct solana_sdk::commitment_config::CommitmentConfig")
, ) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html)
, [Error](../client_error/struct.ClientError.html "struct solana_client::client_error::ClientError")
\>

Returns the stake minimum delegation, in lamports, based on the commitment level.

##### [§](#rpc-reference-68)
RPC Reference

This method corresponds directly to the [`getStakeMinimumDelegation`](https://solana.com/docs/rpc/http/getstakeminimumdelegation)
 RPC method.

##### [§](#examples-78)
Examples

    let stake_minimum_delegation =
    rpc_client.get_stake_minimum_delegation_with_commitment(CommitmentConfig::confirmed())?;

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1.13.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/either/into_either.rs.html#29)
[§](#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1.13.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1.13.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1.13.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.13.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1.13.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.13.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1.13.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1.13.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1.13.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1.13.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1.13.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.13.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1.13.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.13.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1.13.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [with\_subscriber](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.41/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/tracing/instrument/trait.WithSubscriber.html#method.with_subscriber)
<S>(self, subscriber: S) -> [WithDispatch](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.41/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/tracing/instrument/struct.WithDispatch.html "struct tracing::instrument::WithDispatch")
<Self>

where S: [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")
<[Dispatch](https://docs.rs/tracing-core/0.1.33/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/tracing_core/dispatcher/struct.Dispatch.html "struct tracing_core::dispatcher::Dispatch")
\>,

Attaches the provided [`Subscriber`](https://docs.rs/tracing-core/0.1.33/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/tracing_core/subscriber/trait.Subscriber.html "trait tracing_core::subscriber::Subscriber")
 to this type, returning a [`WithDispatch`](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.41/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/tracing/instrument/struct.WithDispatch.html "struct tracing::instrument::WithDispatch")
 wrapper. [Read more](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.41/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/tracing/instrument/trait.WithSubscriber.html#method.with_subscriber)

 wrapper. [Read more](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.41/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/tracing/instrument/trait.WithSubscriber.html#method.with_current_subscriber)
